danger_signals.meaning: collision hazard; bumping opponent causes them to crash
impassable barriers; driving off-track results in crash/death

safety_signals.visual_form: buggy sprite on-screen with normal appearance
empty road ahead with no opponent vehicles
safety_signals.meaning: safe passage; no immediate collision risk

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: opponent buggies rendered as distinct sprite variations
multiple buggy sprite designs distinguish different opponent vehicles

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: scrolling track with player and opponent vehicles visible; HUD shows racing position/score
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: buggies move forward; collision detection active; opponent AI drives toward finish

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: opponent buggy sprite flashes or deforms; vehicle may leave track or flip
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: opponent vehicle crashes; player gains positioning advantage or points

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: buggy moves into wall/boundary sprite; potential visual deformation or screen shake
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: loss of control; possible race reset or vehicle damage state

environmental_storytelling.example: continuous scrolling road environment communicates active racing progression and speed sensation
opponent buggy placement on track conveys relative race position and threat proximity
sprite distortion or flash on impact provides immediate communication that bumping is core mechanic

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: moves forward along scrolling track; responds to input controls
autonomous forward movement on track; collision triggers crash behavior
sprite leaves track; vehicle becomes non-threat; visual indication opponent is disabled

notes: Bump 'N' Jump is a combat-racing game where bumping opponents off-road is primary gameplay mechanic. Visual communication centers on: (1) track as threat boundary, (2) opponent vehicles as targets/obstacles, (3) collision as success state. Game uses minimal color and detail; communication relies on sprite placement, scrolling motion, and collision reactions. No documented faction alignment systems or political narrative elements. Regional differences noted (Japan vs US logo/text changes) are cosmetic only and do not affect world-state manifestation.
